signals.headline: **Fed's March 17–18, 2026 FOMC minutes** released April 8, 2026, with no new rate cuts despite raised core PCE inflation
signals.raw: **Fed's March 17–18, 2026 FOMC minutes** released April 8, 2026, with no new rate cuts despite raised core PCE inflation forecast to 2.7% (from 2.5%), citing Middle East oil spikes and sticky pressures; still projects one 2026 cut amid labor market risks[2][7].

signals.headline: **Powell's March 30, 2026 Harvard speech** (pre-term end in May): Fed to "wait and see" on Iran war, tariffs (adding 0
signals.raw: **Powell's March 30, 2026 Harvard speech** (pre-term end in May): Fed to "wait and see" on Iran war, tariffs (adding 0.5–1% to inflation as one-time), and gas prices before moves, committed to 2% target[3].

signals.headline: **Bank of Korea (BoK) pause influenced** by Fed's hawkish hold, avoiding cuts amid energy shocks and weak won to prevent
signals.raw: **Bank of Korea (BoK) pause influenced** by Fed's hawkish hold, avoiding cuts amid energy shocks and weak won to prevent inflation rebound[2][5].

signals.headline: **South Korea inflation stats** stable at ~3
signals.raw: **South Korea inflation stats** stable at ~3.6% in 2023, with high groceries but low utilities; no 2026 annual figure or new data[1].
